name: Aguascalientes
funFact: Aguascalientes hosts the San Marcos Fair, one of Mexico's oldest and largest national fairs.

name: Baja California
funFact: Mexicali sits on the U.S. border and is known for a notable Chinese-Mexican culinary scene.

name: Baja California Sur
funFact: La Paz lies on the Sea of Cortez, which Jacques Cousteau called 'the aquarium of the world'.

name: Campeche
funFact: The walled city of Campeche is a UNESCO World Heritage Site built to repel pirates.

name: Chiapas
funFact: Chiapas contains the ancient Maya ruins of Palenque deep in its rainforest.

name: Chihuahua
funFact: Chihuahua is Mexico's largest state by area and home to the dramatic Copper Canyon.

name: Coahuila
funFact: Saltillo is famous for its colorful traditional sarape blankets.

name: Colima
funFact: Colima is one of Mexico's smallest states and home to an active volcano.

name: Durango
funFact: Durango's desert landscapes made it a popular setting for Hollywood Western films.

name: Guanajuato
funFact: Guanajuato's colorful colonial city sits above a network of underground road tunnels.

name: Guerrero
funFact: Guerrero is home to the famous beach resort of Acapulco.

name: Hidalgo
funFact: Pachuca, a mining town, helped introduce football (soccer) to Mexico via Cornish miners.

name: Jalisco
funFact: Jalisco is the birthplace of both tequila and mariachi music.

name: México
funFact: Toluca is one of the highest-altitude cities in Mexico, near the Nevado de Toluca volcano.

name: Michoacán
funFact: Michoacán's forests host the spectacular overwintering of millions of monarch butterflies.

name: Morelos
funFact: Cuernavaca is nicknamed 'the City of Eternal Spring' for its mild year-round climate.

name: Nayarit
funFact: Nayarit is home to the Huichol people, known for their vivid beadwork and yarn art.

name: Nuevo León
funFact: Monterrey is Mexico's industrial powerhouse, framed by the Cerro de la Silla mountain.

name: Oaxaca
funFact: Oaxaca is famed for its mole sauces, mezcal, and Día de los Muertos celebrations.

name: Puebla
funFact: Puebla is the birthplace of mole poblano and the Cinco de Mayo battle of 1862.

name: Querétaro
funFact: Querétaro's colonial aqueduct has 74 towering arches and is a city symbol.

name: Quintana Roo
funFact: Quintana Roo contains Cancún, Tulum and the Riviera Maya along the Caribbean coast.

name: San Luis Potosí
funFact: San Luis Potosí's surreal Huasteca region features turquoise waterfalls like Tamul.

name: Sinaloa
funFact: Sinaloa is a major agricultural state often called the 'breadbasket of Mexico'.

name: Sonora
funFact: Sonora is home to much of the Sonoran Desert and prized carne asada beef.

name: Tabasco
funFact: Tabasco was a heartland of the ancient Olmec civilization, famed for its colossal stone heads.

name: Tamaulipas
funFact: Tamaulipas borders Texas along the Rio Grande and the Gulf of Mexico.

name: Tlaxcala
funFact: Tlaxcala is Mexico's smallest state and an early ally of the Spanish against the Aztecs.

name: Veracruz
funFact: Veracruz's port was the main gateway between Spain and colonial Mexico.

name: Yucatán
funFact: Yucatán contains Chichén Itzá, one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.

name: Zacatecas
funFact: Zacatecas grew rich on silver and its baroque cathedral is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Mexico City
funFact: Mexico City is a federal entity (CDMX) built atop the Aztec capital of Tenochtitlan.
